reasoning: The 2026 World Cup is already decided: Spain beat Argentina 1-0 after extra time in the July 19 final at MetLife Stadium, with substitute Ferran Torres scoring the winner early in the second period of extra time. This is Spain's second World Cup title and their first since 2010, adding to their Euro 2024 crown under Luis de la Fuente. Spain dominated the final with 20 shots and roughly 1.94 xG, and their run was defined by elite midfield depth (Rodri, Pedri, Zubimendi) and the tournament's best defense, while a 10-man Argentina—reduced by Enzo Fernández's late red card—managed just two shots despite Emi Martínez's record 11 saves. With the trophy already lifted, this forecast reflects a settled outcome rather than a probabilistic projection; Morocco's run to the final eight was the standout outsider story of the tournament.
